description: The absence-CHECK form `@x is not` does not fire E-TYPE-045 (§42.10). `if (@ready is not)` is the canonical postfix absence check, not a prefix negation, so E-TYPE-045 stays silent. The clean twin of e-type-045-prefix-not-negation-pos.
rationale: §42.10 `expr is not` is the sanctioned absence check; E-TYPE-045 fires only on the forbidden PREFIX `not expr` negation form.
